data.title: Code review agents should not run tests, lint, or format checks — reviewer scope is non-deterministic analysis only
data.description:
## What happened

In host projects, code review agents (light, standard, deep) spend time and tokens running tests, lint checks, and format checks during their review pass. These checks are already performed deterministically by the commit workflow (Steps 1-3) and pre-commit hooks. The reviewer is duplicating work that is already gate-enforced.

## Expected behavior

Code review agents should focus exclusively on non-deterministic analysis that cannot be performed by automated tools:
- Logic correctness and edge cases
- Security vulnerabilities
- Design quality and maintainability
- Convention adherence
- Cross-cutting concerns and architectural implications

Tests, lint, and format checks are deterministic gates handled by:
- Step 1 (test-batched.sh)
- Step 2 (ruff format)
- Step 3 (ruff check, mypy)
- Pre-commit hooks (test-gate, format-check)

## Fix needed

1. Add explicit negative directive to reviewer-base.md (shared across all tiers): 'Do NOT run tests, lint checks, or format checks. These are handled by deterministic gates in the commit workflow and pre-commit hooks. Your role is non-deterministic analysis that automated tools cannot perform.'
2. Ensure this directive is inherited by all generated reviewer agents (light, standard, deep-correctness, deep-verification, deep-hygiene, deep-arch)
